content.full_text:
April 23, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

The City of Cupertino is an active participant in the Cupertino Chamber of Commerce through its annual funding of a "Shop Cupertino" program. Because sales tax is a major source of revenues, the City knows it is important to encourage retail sales in the community.

Although the City assists through funding, it does not have control over development and/or implementation of the program.

This year, the Chamber has developed a "member to member discount" program. Chamber members are eligible to receive cards which entitle them to discounts at certain merchant locations. Discounts of many kinds are offered, such as 10% discount on an attorney fee; bowl one game, get one free; discount on purchase of jewelry; discount on a hotel room; discounts at certain restaurants; etc.

The Chamber wants to include city employees in this program. Would this be considered as a "gift" and, therefore, reportable on a Statement of Economic Interest form? Also, for those who are individual members of the Chamber and eligible to participate in the program, would this be a "gift" and reportable? If it is to be reported as a gift, how would its value be determined?

Sincerely,

[signature]

DOROTHY CORNELIUS, CMC
CITY CLERK
